zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Lacy於1417年出生在英國英格蘭越可爾武德頌 李斯時，她的父親John de Lacy23歲，母親Florence Molineux9歲。她於1434年與John Kaye III在英國英格蘭越可爾武德頌 李斯結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。她去世於大約1460年在她的家鄉享年44歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭越可爾武德頌 李斯。

nameMeanings.name: Lacy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Lassy in Calvados, named from a Gaulish personal name Lascius (of uncertain origin and meaning) + the locative suffix -acum.

variants.name: Lasee
meanings: Probably an altered form of English or Irish   Lacy  .

variants.name: Lacey
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Lacy  .

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Lacy was born in 1417, in Woodsome Lees, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, John de Lacy, was 23 and her mother, Florence Molineux, was 9. She married John Kaye III in 1434, in Woodsome Lees, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. She died about 1460, in her hometown, at the age of 44, and was buried in Woodsome Lees, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Lacy est née en 1417, à Woodsome Lees, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John de Lacy, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Florence Molineux, avait 9 ans. Elle a épousé John Kaye III en 1434, à Woodsome Lees, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Elle est décédée environ 1460, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 44 ans, et a été enterrée à Woodsome Lees, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Lacyが1417年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアウッドソメ リーズで生まれた時，父John de Lacyは23歳，母Florence Molineuxは9歳であった。1434年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアウッドソメ リーズでJohn Kaye IIIと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼女は約1460年に故郷で44歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアウッドソメ リーズにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Lacy(이)가 1417년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 우드소 리스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John de Lacy(은)는 23세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Florence Molineux(은)는 9세였다. 그녀는 1434년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 우드소 리스에서 John Kaye III(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1460년 그녀의 고향에서 44세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 우드소 리스에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Lacy родилась в 1417 г. в местности вудсом лись, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени John de Lacy было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Florence Molineux, 9. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Kaye III в 1434 г. в местности вудсом лись, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Она умерла около 1460 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 44 лет и была похоронена в местности вудсом лись, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания.
